prohibitions.rule: The protocol must not become a third creative, Activative, composition, or production authority.
The protocol must not mutate an accepted Visual Asset Demand or completed result in place.
The Visual Asset Editor must not redefine Content Harness semantic, Activative, sequence, composition, identity, continuity, or wrong-reading authority.
The Content Harness must not dictate internal ComfyUI, model, LoRA, candidate, or repair execution through the shared protocol.
The protocol must not duplicate visual-quality evaluation or rank creative amendments.
Unsupported mandatory fields or features must not be silently ignored.
Arbitrary product status strings must not alter shared lifecycle state.
Historical contracts, messages, results, assets, and receipts must not be overwritten.
Idempotent retry and replay must not be treated as the same condition.
Unsigned, corrupted, expired, replayed, or unauthorized messages must not affect protocol state.
Stale, cancelled, superseded, invalidated, or revoked results must not enter current composition without governed revalidation.
Budget Programs, ceilings, quality gates, or required evaluations must not be silently reduced.
Adapters and migrations must not be lossy for semantic, Activative, authority, continuity, composition, or hard-gate fields.
Revoked or invalidated artifacts must not be deleted from historical evidence solely to simplify current state.
Internal VAE production nodes must not become public lifecycle states.
Compatibility must not be claimed solely because JSON parses.
Cancellation must not destructively erase reusable evidence or permit late stale promotion.
A Delegation Set must not erase independent member demand, asset, evaluation, or repair lineage.
The protocol must not authorize constitutional changes that belong to Builder governance.
Implementation must not be authorized without cross-product fixtures and a passing conformance suite.
